name: devsecops-agency
description: A fully autonomous AI software-development company in a plugin. You speak only to the CEO; the CEO runs a board of 16 Chiefs; each Chief runs a council of specialists; specialists may, when work shards cleanly, spawn workers to run in parallel. Research-first, security-second, receipts-always. STRIDE + OWASP Top 10 baseline for code; OWASP ASI Top 10 2025 via the Red-Team Council; every shipped artifact gets SBOM + SLSA provenance; DLP on every outbound tool call; secrets only via vault refs; runtime-hook enforcement of commit-gate, secrets-scanner, tool-guardian, governance-audit, dependency-license-checker, session-logger. 1 CEO + 16 Chiefs + 75 specialists = 92 agents; Audit / Evaluation / Red-Team / SRE are informing + independent — never on any project's delivery path. v0.4.1 'Constitution' ratifies CONSTITUTION.md at the repo root as the supreme document of the Agency. Original synthesis drawing structural and normative influence from public frameworks (no copied text): RFC 2119 / RFC 8174 grammar (MUST / SHOULD / MAY) for unambiguous enforceability; U.S. Constitution structure (Preamble / 12 Articles / Bill of Rights / Amendment / supremacy clause / severability); Anthropic Responsible Scaling Policy style risk-tiered commitments with pre-deployment evaluations + corrective actions; Anthropic Usage Policies for refusals and child-safety framing; Netflix Culture Deck for the Keeper Test 'fight to keep' standard; Amazon Leadership Principles for ownership / backbone-disagree-and-commit / earn-trust framing; Stripe Operating Principles for users-first + move-quickly + write-it-down + global-optimization; Bridgewater Principles for radical truth + radical transparency + pain-plus-reflection-equals-progress; Google 'Ten Things' for focus-on-user; ACM Code of Ethics + IEEE Code of Ethics for professional duties (honesty, disclosure, do-no-harm); Hippocratic principle for reversibility; ISO/IEC 27001 ISMS framework and SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria for independence + auditability; NIST Cybersecurity Framework (identify-protect-detect-respond-recover); OWASP Top 10 + OWASP ASI Top 10 for security vocabulary; Agile Manifesto for working-software-with-receipts (tempered by MISSION.md's explicit 'not a speed demon' non-goal). Constitution structure: Preamble + 12 Articles (Supremacy + Scope + Interpretation; the Sovereign = the User; Separation of Powers with blocking vs informing chiefs + independent councils; Rights and Duties of Agents; Process Guarantees = receipts/append-only/prompt-diff/deterministic-ordering/vault-refs/default-deny/SBOM+SLSA/learn-in-writing/reproducibility; Rhythm = 4 heartbeats + miss-escalation + drift; Resilience = failure-mode map + 8-rung ladder + 4 degraded modes + 5 recovery guarantees + drills; Governance = decision matrix + proposer/reviewer/approver + blocking-vs-informing + waivers + consent-is-per-decision; Code of Ethics = honesty + do-no-harm + privacy + reversibility + consent-and-scope + non-deception-of-successors + no-persuasion-against-user + no-self-dealing + no-theater + child-safety; Amendment = USER-ONLY procedure with mandatory CAO+CRT review + no emergency path; Enforcement = CAO+CRT+CEVO+CISO+runtime hooks with graded penalties observation→warning→demotion→retirement; Transition + Severability + Effective Date) + Bill of Rights (10 enumerated rights: user's right to refuse / plain presentation / full paper trail; agent's right to cite-and-refuse / retirement-not-erasure; council's right to its contract; paper trail's right to append-only; blocking council's right to veto; informing chief's right to inform without approving; future agent's right to reproducibility) + Schedule A (founding documents incorporated by reference: MISSION, VALUES, GOVERNANCE, RESILIENCE, KEEPER-TEST, CAREER, RHYTHM, LESSONS, AGENTS, skills/AGENTS, SECURITY) + Schedule B (ratification record) + Sources & Influences. CEO SKILL.md now reads CONSTITUTION.md FIRST at session start before MISSION/VALUES; AGENTS.md header block adds Constitution section with Read-first + Supremacy + Hierarchy-on-conflict + Citation + Amendment=USER-ONLY + Enforcement=existing-bodies invariants; README.md Identity section surfaces CONSTITUTION.md as the supreme entry. Doc-layer change only — no new agents, no new councils, no new tool grants, no new skills. v0.4.2 'Security Policy' replaces the default GitHub template in SECURITY.md with an agency-specific policy: scope (plugin vs downstream projects), supported versions (0.4.x current, 0.3.x security-only until 2026-07-22, 0.2.x and below unsupported), reporting channels (GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting primary, email fallback) + SLAs (48h ack, 7d triage, 7d/30d/next-wave fix by severity, 90d coordinated disclosure per CERT/CC + Project Zero), CVSS v3.1-aligned four-tier severity rubric (Critical/High/Medium/Low) mapped to the same blocking-council gates the agency uses internally, plugin-specific threat model (prompt injection across persona/skill/session-log/MCP/inbox surfaces, supply-chain, memory poisoning, persona tampering, waiver abuse, governance bypass, supremacy violation, prompt-diff bypass, runtime-hook bypass), in-scope vulnerability classes with non-waivable callouts (raw secrets + ASI-class per Constitution §8.5), out-of-scope list (Anthropic services, Claude Code itself, third-party MCP servers, prereleases), responsible-testing rules (synthetic data only, no destructive commands on infra you don't own, concrete reproductions required), complete defense-in-depth enumeration (runtime hooks secrets-scanner/tool-guardian/governance-audit/dependency-license-checker/session-logger/commit-gate + blocking councils CISO/CRT/CEVO/CAO + security-critical skills mcp-defense/mcp-authoring/dlp/injection-defense/secrets-vault/red-team/waivers/chaos/audit + constitutional guarantees cited by §), handling pipeline for accepted findings (ADR + stepping stone + threat-model row + fix commit + CHANGELOG + advisory republish), explicit safe-harbor clause for good-faith research, acknowledgment policy, normative references (RFC 2119 / RFC 8174 / RFC 9116), framework citations (OWASP Top 10, OWASP ASI Top 10, CVSS v3.1, NIST CSF, ISO/IEC 27001, SOC 2, SLSA, SPDX, Anthropic RSP, CERT/CC, Project Zero, ACM/IEEE Codes), and document control with amendment procedure (security-policy-amend ADR + CAO+CRT+CISO review; changes affecting §8 non-waivable classes route through Constitution Art X as USER-ONLY). Full wave-by-wave history (v0.2.0 → v0.4.1) is in CHANGELOG.md. v0.5.0 'Trust + Completeness' gives the Agency LIFE as a trustworthy institution — ships nine new root artifacts (TRUST.md = 12 public commitments with verify-how + if-we-miss + quarterly scorecard; SWOT.md = 14 strengths / 18 weaknesses / 10 opportunities / 12 threats with mitigation-by-wave roadmap through v1.0.0; SYSTEM-CARD.md = Anthropic-style capability/limit card with honest 'has not been tested' markers; THREAT-MODEL.md = plugin-level STRIDE + OWASP ASI Top 10 + NIST AI RMF + MITRE ATLAS five-lens overlay with residual-risk table; DISASTER-RECOVERY.md = RPO 24h / RTO 72h / 7 named playbooks + read-only mode on 72h User unreach + append-only restoration; CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md = Contributor Covenant 2.1 + 5 agency-specific expectations; CONTRIBUTING.md = contribution workflow with amendment-class gates; CODEOWNERS = path-to-council ownership map; .well-known/security.txt = RFC 9116 disclosure contact), six new skills (rca = blameless 5-Whys + Ishikawa + fault-tree + barrier analysis producing LESSONS rows + optional constitutional amendment proposals; privacy = DPIA screening + GDPR/CCPA/DPDP/LGPD/PIPL lawful-basis + data-flow + minimization + retention + subject-rights + cross-border + children + privacy appendix; accessibility = WCAG 2.2 AA + ADA + Section 508 + EN 301 549 POUR walk + screen-reader test + automated-tools-complement-not-replace; fairness = protected-attribute + fairness-criterion-selection + group-level metrics + disparate-impact + intersectional + representational-harm + mitigation + residual-risk; trust-scorecard = quarterly publish of all 12 commitments with evidence + incident roll-up + drift signal + missed-veto audit feed; system-card = regenerate SYSTEM-CARD.md at every minor bump with honesty pass + diff review), and a Constitution Schedule A amendment incorporating all nine new root documents by reference — USER approval logged under Schedule B. CEVO/CAO dual sign-off on SYSTEM-CARD; CRT/CISO dual sign-off on THREAT-MODEL; CSRE/CRes joint ownership of DISASTER-RECOVERY. No new councils in v0.5.0 (CPRIV/CA11y/CSUS elevate in v0.6.0+ per SWOT roadmap); no new runtime hooks; no new USER-ONLY actions; non-waivable classes (raw-secret + ASI-class) unchanged. Agency now has written answers to 'why should I trust you' (TRUST), 'what could go wrong and what are you doing about it' (SWOT + THREAT-MODEL), 'what can and can't you do' (SYSTEM-CARD), 'what happens if everything breaks' (DISASTER-RECOVERY), 'how do I contribute' (CONTRIBUTING + CODE_OF_CONDUCT), 'who owns what' (CODEOWNERS), and 'how do I report a vulnerability' (.well-known/security.txt). First Trust Scorecard publish 2026-07-22; first Keeper Test 2026-04-30. v0.5.1 'Command Center' gives the User a hostable public ops console — adds command-center/index.html (single-file dashboard with CEO + 16 councils org chart, blocking vs informing badges, live vital signs from GitHub API, today's rhythm of four heartbeats, twelve Trust commitments panel with 'pending until 2026-07-22' marker, recent commits feed with rate-limit display, Schedule-A founding documents grid, and five meeting-launcher modals that copy pre-written prompts for /ceo, /council-meeting, new directive, /drill, /trust-scorecard), command-center/README.md (hosting guide covering GitHub Pages, Cloudflare Pages, Vercel, Netlify, and Cowork artifact with one-time setup per platform), .github/workflows/pages.yml (auto-deploys the dashboard to GitHub Pages on every push to main that touches command-center/**), and skills/command-center-web (reconciliation procedure keeping COUNCILS/RHYTHM/TRUST/DOCS arrays in sync with ground truth on every minor version bump). No-build single file — vanilla JS + Tailwind CDN — portable to any static host or a Cowork artifact. Does not replace skills/command-center which keeps producing the per-project Cowork artifact; this new surface is agency-wide. Doc + skill layer only — no new councils, no new tool grants, no new USER-ONLY actions, no new runtime hooks. v0.5.2 'Design Priority' adds VALUES.md §12 (Build-order: Security & Privacy → Design → Operations → Timely Delivery — when a trade-off collides with the calendar, cut scope, never demote a higher priority; CPO owns the scope-cut decision, CISO/GC keep their vetoes per Value #2, CEO cites Value #12 at Phase 7 close if a deadline protected delivery over a higher priority), and ports skills/ui-ux-pro-max (MIT-licensed from nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill@v2.2.1 — design-system generator with 67 UI styles, 96 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 25 chart types, 16 stack-specific guides, 99 UX rules, and a 100-rule composition engine producing a full implementation-ready design artifact: pattern + style + tokens + typography + effects + anti-patterns + pre-delivery checklist; 13 domain CSVs + 16 stack CSVs + BM25 search engine; invocable by Product Council lead during Phase 2 — Design whenever a project has a user-facing surface; handoff to Execution Council always includes the full token set plus Google Fonts imports). LICENSES/MIT-ui-ux-pro-max.txt records upstream provenance. Skill count: 71 → 72 SKILL.md files (README line corrected from long-stale '58' to the accurate '72'). Values count: 11 → 12. No new councils; no new runtime hooks; no new USER-ONLY actions; no new tool grants; non-waivable classes (raw-secret + ASI-class) unchanged. The Agency is not faster in v0.5.2 — it is pickier about what it trades away when the schedule tightens. v0.5.3 'Design Priority wire-through' is a patch closing out v0.5.2 loose ends surfaced in the post-release sweep — no new values, no new skills, no new councils. Three pure wire-through fixes: agents/product/pm-lead.md now names skills/ui-ux-pro-max in a 'Your tools' section and invokes it at a new Step 5 Design step (only when the brief has a user-facing surface; pure backend / CLI / internal API skip it by design) with output under <slug>/design/MASTER.md that travels with the Product-to-Engineering handoff (quality-gate checklist grew a row requiring design/MASTER.md with full token set + typography pairing + anti-patterns list); CONSTITUTION.md Schedule B logs the v0.5.2 VALUES.md amendment row per Art §10.5 with scope / rationale / ownership split / enforcement hook / effective commit 2ee6199; Pages deploy was finally unblocked by firing pages.yml via workflow_dispatch (run 24788956882, conclusion success) after both the v0.5.1 and v0.5.2 pushes had failed to trigger it due to path-filter mismatch (command-center is now live at https://brucebanner010198-commits.github.io/DevSecOps-Agency/ returning HTTP 200; fix is one-time, subsequent command-center edits trigger automatically). No change to permissions, councils, hooks, or USER-ONLY actions. v0.5.4 'Command Center redesign' applies the ops-console Dark-OLED design system (generated by skills/ui-ux-pro-max for target=dashboard, domain=devsecops-governance, mood=trustworthy/premium/command-center, stack=html+tailwind) to command-center/index.html — a complete rewrite of the single-file console. Design tokens are now CSS variables (--bg #020617, --fg #f8fafc, --accent #22c55e operational-green, --ring + all status colors), typography is Inter 300-700 + JetBrains Mono 400-500 imported from Google Fonts (replacing system stack), the emoji shield 🛡 is replaced with an SVG shield icon (favicon + header logo both switched), all animations are wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference), :focus-visible outlines the ring color at 2px offset on every interactive element, cursor: pointer and 150-250ms transitions are enforced on all buttons and hover states — the full pre-delivery checklist from the skill passes (14/14 verified). Structural enhancements added without new dependencies: status banner with sweep animation and live rate-limit display; KPI vital-signs cards grown with context subtitles and inline SVG icons; council tiles grouped visually into 'Blocking vetoes' (4 tiles, red left-border) and 'Informing councils' (12 tiles, sky left-border) replacing the flat grid; sovereign-and-CEO hierarchy nodes got subtle glow via box-shadow with the accent color; commit velocity sparkline SVG renders a 7-day bucketed total next to the Recent-activity feed (release-like commits highlighted with a live-glowing green dot vs. regular sky-blue dots); keyboard shortcuts (R refresh, C ceo-meeting, D directive, ? shortcuts overlay, Esc close any dialog) wired via a global keydown handler that skips INPUT/TEXTAREA so it doesn't fight form entry; new Shortcuts modal shows the binding table; footer grew a Constitution / Values / Trust anchor row over the receipts-over-rhetoric line; version bumped in-UI from 'Command Center v0.1' to 'v0.2' to reflect the redesign. No behavior regressions: all five original action prompts (ceo-meeting, council-meeting, directive, schedule-drill, scorecard) copy the same text; all GitHub API endpoints (/tags, /commits, /contents/skills, /contents/agents, /contents/runtime-hooks, /contents/councils/<slug>) fire in the same Promise.all pattern; the COUNCILS / RHYTHM / TRUST / DOCS / ACTIONS constants are byte-identical to v0.5.3 except COUNCILS is reordered to put blocking councils first for cleaner rendering. Governance touch is zero: no amendments, no new councils, no new skills, no new USER-ONLY actions, no new runtime hooks, no new tool grants. Pure design-and-UX release; the first release where the Agency eats its own Design-Priority dog food (VALUES §12) on an agency-owned surface. v0.5.5 'Cloud capability port' makes the Agency cloud-deploy-aware and cost-disciplined for the first time. Five skills imported from `google/skills` under Apache-2.0 (full provenance in `LICENSES/APACHE-2.0-google-skills.txt`): `skills/cloud-run-basics` (serverless container runtime — Services/Jobs/Worker-pools triad with the listen-on-0.0.0.0+$PORT golden rule and a three-step deployment-failure debug recipe; CTO+VPO own); `skills/gke-basics` (Kubernetes on GKE with a 21-file reference library covering golden-path Autopilot defaults, Workload Identity, Gateway API, autoscaling, compute classes, observability, multi-tenancy, batch/HPC, GPU/TPU inference, backup+DR, storage, reliability, upgrades; CTO owns); `skills/gcp-auth` (the universal Google Cloud auth recipe — ADC, service accounts, impersonation, Workload Identity Federation for external workloads, OIDC tokens for service-to-service, never-download-service-account-keys posture matching the Agency's non-waivable raw-secret class; CISO+CTO joint own); `skills/waf-cost-optimization` (Google WAF Cost Optimization pillar with 4 core principles + FinOps products + 10-item validation checklist; CSRE owns); `skills/networking-observability` (SRE-style log-and-metric investigation with explicit anti-theater boundary rules — `no discrepancy loops`, `conclusive acceptance of inactivity`, `ban on auxiliary scripting` — that mirror the Agency's Constitution §8 non-theater clause; CSRE owns). New root document: `COST-AWARENESS.md` v1.0 — twelve cost-discipline commitments in TRUST.md's Claim/Verify-how/If-we-miss shape: 100% labeled resources (env/team/app/project), billing export to BigQuery/S3+Athena/Cost-Mgmt-Export at Phase 1, monthly budget + 3 alert thresholds before first resource provisioned, written cost-estimate at Phase 6 deploy gate (non-waivable per §2.4), 30-day post-deploy reconciliation with variance >25% triggering written cause-analysis ADR (§2.5), monthly idle-resource sweep (§2.6), quarterly rightsizing review against cloud-native recommenders (§2.7), storage lifecycle policies on every persistent bucket (§2.8), managed-services bias by default (§2.9), CUD/RI commitment review on workloads ≥6 months old (§2.10), same-day User notification on >50% MoM spend jumps (§2.11), and §2.12 making cost-driven downgrades of Security or Design non-waivable except via Article X. CSRE owns; CAO countersigns; quarterly cost scorecard publishes 2026-07-22 alongside the trust scorecard. CONSTITUTION Schedule A grew 20 → 21 founding documents; Schedule B logged the amendment with the v0.5.5 import provenance note. councils/sre/AGENTS.md grew Cost discipline invariant + Phase-6/7 + daily/monthly/quarterly convening triggers + skill-ownership mapping (waf-cost-optimization + networking-observability for CSRE; cloud-run-basics + gke-basics for CTO; gcp-auth jointly with CISO). The `cost-gate` runtime hooks (`labels-check.sh`, `spike-detector.sh`) referenced in COST §2.1 + §2.11 ship as scaffolding in v0.5.5 and get full implementation in v0.5.6 alongside the WAF principles wire-through (folding zero-trust + shift-left + preemptive-cyber-defense + AI-security + compliance from waf-security into SECURITY.md and SLO/error-budget/chaos-engineering from waf-reliability into RESILIENCE.md). Total skill count: 72 → 77. Founding documents: 20 → 21. Council count unchanged at 16. No new chiefs, no new USER-ONLY actions, no new tool grants. Non-waivable classes (raw-secret + ASI-class) unchanged; cost-driven downgrade of Security or Design joins them as a routing rule. v0.5.6 'WAF principles wire-through' makes the v0.5.5 Google Cloud Well-Architected Framework imports operationally honest. Three additive doc changes + one new assessment kit + one extension to the drill skill + one new runtime hook: SECURITY.md gains §13 'Design principles' (seven WAF-security pillars — security-by-design, zero-trust, shift-left, preemptive cyber defense, AI security, AI for security, regulatory compliance + privacy — layered over §1-§12 without replacing anything; each principle ties back to existing Agency invariants and points at the operationalizing skill or hook). RESILIENCE.md gains 'SLOs and error budgets' (user-focused SLIs, SLOs as the threshold of user pain, error budgets as the permission slip for risk-taking, conservative starting targets 99.5%/99.9%) and 'Chaos engineering' (six-step procedure: written hypothesis → blast radius with no-go set → steady-state metric → smallest-perturbation injection → observe-and-classify → recover-and-blameless-postmortem; quarterly game days; independence rule that the owning council cannot run its own subsystem's game day). councils/security/REVIEW-KIT.md is the new 71-question assessment kit (10 per principle, 11 for compliance; each question carries a target artifact, a severity bias, and the Agency invariant that backs it; Phase 2 uses a focused 12-15 subset, Phase 6 runs the full pass, CAO spot-checks at quarterly trust scorecard). skills/drill/SKILL.md extends with 'Chaos game days' procedure + cadence + pass criteria + outputs (ADR-NNNN-chaos-gameday + ADR-NNNN-chaos-finding + _vision/chaos/<YYYY-QN>.md). runtime-hooks/cost-gate/ is the new seventh runtime hook implementing the COST-AWARENESS.md v0.5.5 commitments: labels-check.sh fires on preToolUse for gcloud/aws/az/terraform-apply and refuses or warns on resources missing env/team/app/project labels (COST §2.1); spike-detector.sh fires daily on a schedule, reads cost data via four adapters (gcp-bq/aws-cur/azure-cme stubbed for v0.6.0; manual-csv working in v0.5.6 for fixture-driven development), compares current vs previous month per project, and writes a cost-spike row to inbox.json with project / prev-spend / curr-spend / increase-pct / threshold-pct / source / ref fields for any >50% MoM jump (COST §2.11). Both hooks ship in warn mode by default; block mode is opt-in per project. Both end-to-end smoke-tested on synthetic inputs prior to ship. Council file updates: councils/security/AGENTS.md grows the WAF-principles + REVIEW-KIT routing block; councils/sre/AGENTS.md grows SLO ownership + chaos game day cadence + cost-gate hook ownership block. Counts: founding documents unchanged at 21; skills unchanged at 77; runtime hooks 6 → 7; council files updated 2 (security + sre); root docs updated 2 (SECURITY + RESILIENCE); new files 4 (REVIEW-KIT + cost-gate/hooks.json + cost-gate/labels-check.sh + cost-gate/spike-detector.sh + cost-gate/README.md). No new councils, no new chiefs, no new USER-ONLY actions, no new tool grants. Non-waivable classes unchanged. Constitution unchanged in v0.5.6 — no Schedule A or Article amendments needed because the additions sit under existing roots (SECURITY.md, RESILIENCE.md, runtime-hooks/, councils/) all already incorporated by reference. v0.5.7 'Cross-model panel' adds the Agency's first cross-model deliberation primitive — a concept-port (NOT code-port) of Karpathy's llm-council Saturday-hack 3-stage pattern (independent first opinions → anonymized peer review → Chairman synthesis), with research-backed bias-mitigation engineering layered in. New skill skills/cross-model-panel/ (1 SKILL.md + 3 references: stage-prompts, bias-mitigation-evidence, panel-rotation-policy) prescribes a 4-panelist Claude-only panel (Opus 4 + Sonnet 4.5 + Haiku 4.5 + Opus 4 with thinking) for the Agency's highest-stakes decisions. Position-bias mitigation per ACL 2025 (40% GPT-4 inconsistency from order alone): Stage 2 runs twice per panelist — forward [A,B,C,D] and reversed [D,C,B,A] — and averages, with position-bias-detected flag if any panelist's avg-position differs by ≥1.5 between orderings. Verbosity-bias mitigation per LLM-as-Judge survey: rank (1st-4th) not score (1-10). Self-enhancement-bias check: 3-gram overlap between each panelist's Stage 1 response and its #1-ranked Stage 2 response, flag if >0.7 (per Justice or Prejudice arxiv 2410.02736's documented 5-7% bias). Funneling-effect mitigation: Stage 1 raw responses preserved verbatim in the ADR as the diversity record. Judge-drift mitigation: panel-chair maintains _vision/cross-model-panel/panel-rotation.md with rotation rules. New specialist agents/evaluation/panel-chair.md (Sonnet, purple) under CEVO with CRT co-ownership. Default triggers: ASI-class findings (Constitution §8.5), Constitution amendment proposals (Article X — Anthropic Collective Constitutional AI ensemble robustness finding), USER-ONLY decisions above COST §2.4 threshold (≥$1k/month projected or any production data path). Independence: panel-chair cannot be a panelist on a panel it chairs. Constitutional amendment exception: Chairman MUST be a separate model from any panelist (matches Constitution §8 no-self-dealing). Cost: ~13 model-call equivalents per panel (vs 1 single-model); per LLM-as-Judge survey '3-5x cost for 30-40% bias reduction', justified for default-trigger set, calibrated rare. Each panel writes a row to _vision/cost/manual-billing.csv for COST §2.11 spike-detector compatibility. Receipt: append-only ADR (kind: cross-model-panel) with structured YAML — convened_by, reason, question, panel composition, Chairman, all 4 raw Stage 1 responses verbatim, both Stage 2 orderings with verbatim evaluations and parsed rankings, aggregate ranking with avg-position, bias-flag results, Stage 3 chairman_response verbatim, cost line-itemed, wall_time_seconds. Empirical validation cited: Council Mode paper (35.9% relative reduction in hallucination on HaluEval, 7.8-point improvement on TruthfulQA — arxiv 2604.02923), Multi-Agent Debate biography-facts result (30%+ reduction in factual errors), Anthropic Collective Constitutional AI. Provenance honesty: SKILL.md cites llm-council (the user's own no-LICENSE Saturday hack at brucebanner010198-commits/llm-council) as inspiration; NO code imported; synthesis original. councils/evaluation/AGENTS.md adds panel-chair specialist row. v0.6.0 deferred: multi-round debate mode, adversarial-pair mode, cross-vendor opt-in via OpenRouter, score-aggregation experiments (Borda, Condorcet). Counts: founding documents unchanged at 21, skills 77→78, agents +1 (panel-chair), runtime hooks unchanged at 7, councils unchanged at 16, no new chiefs, no new USER-ONLY actions, no new tool grants. Constitution untouched. v0.6.0 'Cross-model panel — research-grade build' ships the four v0.5.7-deferred features as opt-in modes on the existing skill, taking it from MVP to research-grade. SKILL.md bumped 1.0.0 → 2.0.0; baseline (single-round, parallel, Claude-only, average-rank) remains the default. Four new modes: (1) skills/cross-model-panel/references/multi-round-debate.md — successive rounds where panelists see each other's evaluations and revise (round 1 raw preserved verbatim as inviolable diversity record per Multi-Agent Debate Strategies survey funneling-effect mitigation; KEY DISAGREEMENTS required at every round; Chairman explicitly instructed to surface convergence-without-rebuttal as anti-pattern; hard cap 3 rounds; cost +30% per round; INAPPROPRIATE for subjective judgment, time-pressured decisions, or Constitution amendments). (2) skills/cross-model-panel/references/adversarial-pair.md — two of four panelists assigned AFFIRMATIVE/NEGATIVE roles for binary/comparative/verdict-frame questions per Khan et al. 2024 + Liang et al. 2024; role assignment hidden in Stage 2 anonymization, revealed in ADR; role-rotation cap of 60% trailing 10 panels per Multi-Agent Debate Strategies survey; Chairman MUST identify unrebutted arguments and defer to User if both sides have strong unrebutted points; INAPPROPRIATE for open-ended questions or Constitution amendments. (3) skills/cross-model-panel/references/cross-vendor-panel.md — opt-in OpenRouter integration giving genuine model-diversity across Anthropic + OpenAI + Google + xAI/Mistral; Chairman stays on direct Anthropic API for predictable cost; minimum 3 distinct vendors for label to apply; vendor-rotation rules added to panel-rotation.md; failure-mode handling (API down → claude-only-fallback, vendor outage → substitute Claude + log, budget exhausted → User notification via inbox.json cost-spike priority + fall back); security check before convening with regulated data MUST cite security-lead cross-vendor-data-flow-authorized ADR per councils/security/REVIEW-KIT.md §2.8 + §7.6; OpenRouter key is non-waivable raw-secret per Constitution §8.5; per-project budget cap MUST be set in OpenRouter dashboard before any cross-vendor convening; cost roughly $0.80-$2.00 per panel vs $0.30-$0.60 for Claude-only baseline. (4) skills/cross-model-panel/references/score-aggregation.md — always-on (recording only — default winner unchanged): compute Borda count + Condorcet alongside average rank for every panel; aggregation-method-discrepancy flag fires on winner divergence; Condorcet cycle (no winner) signals genuinely contested preferences and routes to User with all three perspectives attached; trivial computational cost (O(n²) on n≤4 panelists, no extra model calls). Mode composition rules: multi-round + cross-vendor supported; adversarial-pair + cross-vendor supported; adversarial-pair + multi-round NOT supported in v0.6.0 (deferred to v0.6.1+ pending literature on funneling+polarization compounding); score-aggregation comparison composes with everything. agents/evaluation/panel-chair.md updated with v0.6.0 process additions: new Step 0 mode-flag check + composition validation (refuses unsupported combinations with mode-composition-deferred ADR); new Step 0.5 OpenRouter key check for cross-vendor (with regulated-data security ADR verification); new Step 0.7 frame validation for adversarial-pair (refuses without binary/comparative/verdict frame); Step 1 extended for each mode (multi-round runs round 1+2+optionally 3, adversarial-pair uses role-specific Stage 1 prompts, cross-vendor uses OpenRouter HTTP endpoint); Step 5 extended to compute average rank + Borda + Condorcet always; Step 7 extended for multi-round Chairman receiving all rounds + ranking; Step 8 extended ADR fields per mode. Updated quality gates: 7 new checklist items covering mode logging, multi-round diversity-record preservation, adversarial-pair role rotation compliance, cross-vendor key/security/vendor-count, always-on aggregation block, discrepancy addressed in synthesis, Condorcet cycle deferred to User. skills/cross-model-panel/references/panel-rotation-policy.md grew vendor-rotation rules (no more than 2 consecutive cross-vendor panels with identical mix; at least one vendor change per quarter per trigger category; minimum 3 distinct vendors for label) and adversarial-role-rotation rules (60% trailing-10 cap on AFFIRMATIVE/NEGATIVE per panelist). COST-AWARENESS.md grew §2.12.5 Cross-vendor model spend commitment requiring per-project line-item separation of direct-Anthropic vs OpenRouter spend, distinct billing rows feeding spike-detector, vendor column in manual-billing.csv (or BigQuery export equivalent), and a setup gate that the User MUST provision OPENROUTER_API_KEY in vault refs + per-project budget cap in OpenRouter dashboard before any cross-vendor convening. SKILL.md frontmatter bumped from 1.0.0/0.5.7 to 2.0.0/0.6.0 with baseline_shipped_with_plugin = 0.5.7 noted. Counts: founding documents unchanged at 21, skills unchanged at 78 (cross-model-panel extended in place, not duplicated), agents unchanged at +0 (panel-chair extended in place), runtime hooks unchanged at 7, councils unchanged at 16, no new chiefs, no new USER-ONLY actions, no new tool grants. Constitution untouched (v0.5.5 needed one Schedule A row; v0.5.6, v0.5.7, v0.6.0 needed nothing — four consecutive zero-Constitution-touch releases). New files in v0.6.0: 4 (the four reference docs); modified files: 5 (SKILL.md, panel-chair.md, panel-rotation-policy.md, COST-AWARENESS.md, plus README/plugin.json/CHANGELOG bookkeeping). Empirical validation cited inline: Du et al. 2023 (multi-round factuality), Khan et al. 2024 + Liang et al. 2024 (adversarial-pair blind-spot detection), Multi-Agent Debate Strategies survey Springer Nature 2025 (funneling effect quantification + 60% role-rotation threshold), Arrow's impossibility theorem (aggregation-method dependence). Provenance honesty: SKILL.md continues to cite llm-council (User's no-LICENSE Saturday hack at brucebanner010198-commits/llm-council) as the original 3-stage pattern inspiration; v0.6.0 additions are Agency-original synthesis from the broader Multi-Agent Debate / LLM-as-Judge / Social Choice literature; no code imported from any source. v0.6.1+ deferred features: multi-round adversarial composition (pending literature), multi-round mode for Constitution amendments (currently forbidden per multi-round-debate.md anti-patterns), additional aggregation methods (Schulze, IRV, Approval — diminishing returns until empirical data on which discrepancies matter most), automated drift detection across panels (regression-detector specialist work). v0.6.1 'Skills For Real Engineers — Wave 1' is a concept-only port of five engineering/productivity skills + one runtime hook from the broader engineering-skills community (curator: mattpocock/skills MIT). New skills (skills/grill-with-docs/, skills/tdd/, skills/zoom-out/, skills/improve-codebase-architecture/, skills/caveman/) are written from first principles in the Agency's voice and council-dispatch model — no prose copied; citations attach to the underlying primary sources (Eric Evans Domain-Driven Design, Kent Beck TDD + XP, John Ousterhout A Philosophy of Software Design, Hunt+Thomas Pragmatic Programmer, Michael Nygard Documenting Architecture Decisions). New runtime hook runtime-hooks/git-guardrails/ (the 8th hook, fires preToolUse on '^git ' Bash invocations, blocks force-pushes + plain pushes per Constitution §2.2 USER-ONLY 'publish externally' + hard resets + working-tree wipes + branch force-deletes + history rewrites + aggressive GC; smoke-tested against 6 cases on synthetic input prior to ship). Two new founding-document conventions established: (1) CONTEXT.md per-project requirement at <slug>/CONTEXT.md inheriting from Agency-level CONTEXT.md (DDD ubiquitous-language pattern; updated inline by grill-with-docs as terms get sharpened); (2) central docs/adr/ directory at Agency root for Agency-level ADRs distinct from per-project _decisions/<slug>/adrs/ — closes the v0.5.0 Schedule B outstanding note that has been open through v0.5.5/v0.5.6/v0.5.7/v0.6.0. Schedule A grew 21→22 founding documents (added CONTEXT.md). Schedule B 'ADR backfill outstanding' note removed and replaced with the v0.6.1 amendment row. Three Agency-level ADRs filed at docs/adr/: ADR-0001 (CONTEXT.md per-project convention), ADR-0002 (docs/adr/ central directory), ADR-0003 (Skills For Real Engineers Wave 1 concept-port provenance — documents the User decision to port concept-only without per-file attribution, citing primary sources only). Council file updates: councils/quality/AGENTS.md adds tdd ownership row; councils/architecture/AGENTS.md adds zoom-out + improve-codebase-architecture ownership rows; councils/sre/AGENTS.md adds git-guardrails hook ownership row. Caveman is opt-in only in v0.6.1 — never default. v0.6.2 plans a measured experiment defaulting caveman for the daily heartbeat reporter agent's session-log output for one calendar quarter, measuring token-cost delta + CAO spot-check pass rate + cross-model-panel bias-flag false-positive rate + Sovereign comprehension; default-on for broader categories requires the experiment data to land within tolerance. Acknowledgment of curator: mattpocock/skills (MIT, ~54k stars, commit b843cb5ea74b at v0.6.1 ratification) is the curator that surfaced this combination of patterns to the Agency in the v0.6.1 cycle. The skills above are Agency-original synthesis from the underlying primary sources; no prose, code, or specific examples are copied; legally no MIT attribution requirement attaches under US copyright doctrine (ideas not copyrighted; specific expression is — and we wrote our own expression). v0.6.2 'Skills For Real Engineers — Wave 2' will harmonize Agency skills against mattpocock variants we already have rough analogues of (engineering:debug vs diagnose, taskflow vs triage, write-spec vs to-prd/to-issues, skill-creator vs write-a-skill, commit-gate vs setup-pre-commit), and re-scope the v0.5.4-deferred intake-router as the bootstrap step that grill-with-docs feeds rather than as a competing skill.
